{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "1: // Copyright 2018 ETH Zurich and University of Bologna.
2: // Copyright and related rights are licensed under the Solderpad Hardware
3: // License, Version 0.51 (the "License"); you may not use this file except in
4: // compliance with the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
5: // http://solderpad.org/licenses/SHL-0.51. Unless required by applicable law
6: // or agreed to in writing, software, hardware and materials distributed under
7: // this License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR
8: // CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the License for the
9: // specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.
10: //
11: // Author: Florian Zaruba, ETH Zurich
12: // Date: 05.05.2017
13: // Description: CSR Register File as specified by RISC-V
14: 
15: import ariane_pkg::*;
16: 
17: module csr_regfile #(
18:     parameter int          ASID_WIDTH      = 1,
19:     parameter int unsigned NR_COMMIT_PORTS = 2
20: ) (
21:     input  logic                  clk_i,                      // Clock
22:     input  logic                  rst_ni,                     // Asynchronous reset active low
23:     input  logic                  time_irq_i,                 // Timer threw a interrupt
24:     // send a flush request out if a CSR with a side effect has changed (e.g. written)
25:     output logic                  flush_o,
26:     output logic                  halt_csr_o,                 // halt requested
27:     // commit acknowledge
28:     input  scoreboard_entry_t [NR_COMMIT_PORTS-1:0] commit_instr_i, // the instruction we want to commit
29:     input  logic [NR_COMMIT_PORTS-1:0]              commit_ack_i,   // Commit acknowledged a instruction -> increase instret CSR
30:     // Core and Cluster ID
31:     input  logic  [63:0]          boot_addr_i,                // Address from which to start booting, mtvec is set to the same address
32:     input  logic  [63:0]          hart_id_i,                  // Hart id in a multicore environment (reflected in a CSR)
33:     // we are taking an exception
34:     input exception_t             ex_i,                       // We've got an exception from the commit stage, take its
35: 
36:     input  fu_op                  csr_op_i,                   // Operation to perform on the CSR file
37:     input  logic  [11:0]          csr_addr_i,                 // Address of the register to read/write
38:     input  logic  [63:0]          csr_wdata_i,                // Write data in
39:     output logic  [63:0]          csr_rdata_o,                // Read data out
40:     input  logic                  dirty_fp_state_i,           // Mark the FP sate as dirty
41:     input  logic                  csr_write_fflags_i,         // Write fflags register e.g.: we are retiring a floating point instruction
42:     input  logic  [63:0]          pc_i,                       // PC of instruction accessing the CSR
43:     output exception_t            csr_exception_o,            // attempts to access a CSR without appropriate privilege
44:                                                               // level or to write  a read-only register also
45:                                                               // raises illegal instruction exceptions.
46:     // Interrupts/Exceptions
47:     output logic  [63:0]          epc_o,                      // Output the exception PC to PC Gen, the correct CSR (mepc, sepc) is set accordingly
48:     output logic                  eret_o,                     // Return from exception, set the PC of epc_o
49:     output logic  [63:0]          trap_vector_base_o,         // Output base of exception vector, correct CSR is output (mtvec, stvec)
50:     output riscv::priv_lvl_t      priv_lvl_o,                 // Current privilege level the CPU is in
51:     // FPU
52:     output riscv::xs_t            fs_o,   
[TRUNCATED]

{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "560:             if (trap_to_priv_lvl == riscv::PRIV_LVL_S) begin
561:                 // update sstatus
562:                 mstatus_d.sie  = 1'b0;
563:                 mstatus_d.spie = mstatus_q.sie;
564:                 // this can either be user or supervisor mode
565:                 mstatus_d.spp  = priv_lvl_q[0];
566:                 // set cause
567:                 scause_d       = ex_i.cause;
568:                 // set epc
569:                 sepc_d         = pc_i;
570:                 // set mtval or stval
571:                 stval_d        = (ariane_pkg::ZERO_TVAL
572:                                   && (ex_i.cause inside {
573:                                     riscv::ILLEGAL_INSTR,
574:                                     riscv::BREAKPOINT,
575:                                     riscv::ENV_CALL_UMODE,
576:                                     riscv::ENV_CALL_SMODE,
577:                                     riscv::ENV_CALL_MMODE
578:                                   } || ex_i.cause[63])) ? '0 : ex_i.tval;
579:             // trap to machine mode
580:             end else begin
581:                 // update mstatus
582:                 mstatus_d.mie  = 1'b0;
583:                 mstatus_d.mpie = mstatus_q.mie;
584:                 // save the previous privilege mode
585:                 mstatus_d.mpp  = priv_lvl_q;
586:                 mcause_d       = ex_i.cause;
587:                 // set epc
588:                 mepc_d         = pc_i;
589:                 // set mtval or stval
590:                 mtval_d        = (ariane_pkg::ZERO_TVAL
591:                                   && (ex_i.cause inside {
592:                                     riscv::ILLEGAL_INSTR,
593:                                     riscv::BREAKPOINT,
594:                                     riscv::ENV_CALL_UMODE,
595:                                     riscv::ENV_CALL_SMODE,
596:                                     riscv::ENV_CALL_MMODE
597:                                   } || ex_i.cause[63])) ? '0 : ex_i.tval;
598:             end
599: 
600:             priv_lvl_d = trap_to_priv_lvl;
601:         end
602: 
603:         // ------------------------------
604:         // Debug
605:         // ------------------------------
606:         // Explains why Debug Mode was entered.
607:         // When there are multiple reasons to enter Debug Mode in a single cycle, hardware should set cause to the cause with the highest priority.
608:         // 1: An ebreak instruction was executed. (priority 3)
609:         // 2: The Trigger Module caused a breakpoint exception. (priority 4)
610:         // 3: The debugger requested entry to Debug Mode. (priority 2)
611:         // 4: The hart single stepped because step was set. (priority 1)
612:         // we are currently not in debug mode and could potentially enter
613:         if (!debug_mode_q) begin
614:             dcsr_d.prv = priv_lvl_o;
615:             // trigger module fired
616: 
617:             // caused by a breakpoint
618:             if (ex_i.valid && ex_i.cause == riscv::BREAKPOINT) begin
619:                 // check that we actually want to enter debug depending on the privilege level we are currently in
620:                 unique case (priv_lvl_o)
621:                     riscv::PRIV_LVL_M: begin
622:                         debug_mode_d = dcsr_q.ebreakm;
623:                         set_debug_pc_o = dcsr_q.ebreakm;
624:                     end
625:                     riscv::PRIV_LVL_S: begin
626:                         debug_mode_d = dcsr_q.ebreaks;
627:                         set_debug_pc_o = dcsr_q.ebreaks;
628:                     end
629:                     riscv::PRIV_LVL_U: begin
630:                         debug_mode_d = dcsr_q.ebreaku;
631:                         set_debug_pc_o = dcsr_q.ebreaku;
632:                     end
633:             
[TRUNCATED]

320:     localparam int unsigned IRQ_M_EXT   = 11;
321: 
322:     localparam logic [63:0] MIP_SSIP = (1 << IRQ_S_SOFT);
323:     localparam logic [63:0] MIP_MSIP = (1 << IRQ_M_SOFT);
324:     localparam logic [63:0] MIP_STIP = (1 << IRQ_S_TIMER);
325:     localparam logic [63:0] MIP_MTIP = (1 << IRQ_M_TIMER);
326:     localparam logic [63:0] MIP_SEIP = (1 << IRQ_S_EXT);
327:     localparam logic [63:0] MIP_MEIP = (1 << IRQ_M_EXT);
328: 
329:     localparam logic [63:0] S_SW_INTERRUPT    = (1 << 63) | IRQ_S_SOFT;
330:     localparam logic [63:0] M_SW_INTERRUPT    = (1 << 63) | IRQ_M_SOFT;
331:     localparam logic [63:0] S_TIMER_INTERRUPT = (1 << 63) | IRQ_S_TIMER;
332:     localparam logic [63:0] M_TIMER_INTERRUPT = (1 << 63) | IRQ_M_TIMER;
333:     localparam logic [63:0] S_EXT_INTERRUPT   = (1 << 63) | IRQ_S_EXT;
334:     localparam logic [63:0] M_EXT_INTERRUPT   = (1 << 63) | IRQ_M_EXT;
335: 
336:     // -----
337:     // CSRs
338:     // -----
339:     typedef enum logic [11:0] {
340:         // Floating-Point CSRs
341:         CSR_FFLAGS         = 12'h001,
342:         CSR_FRM            = 12'h002,
343:         CSR_FCSR           = 12'h003,
344:         CSR_FTRAN          = 12'h800,
345:         // Supervisor Mode CSRs
346:         CSR_SSTATUS        = 12'h100,
347:         CSR_SIE            = 12'h104,
348:         CSR_STVEC          = 12'h105,
349:         CSR_SCOUNTEREN     = 12'h106,
350:         CSR_SSCRATCH       = 12'h140,
351:         CSR_SEPC           = 12'h141,
352:         CSR_SCAUSE         = 12'h142,
353:         CSR_STVAL          = 12'h143,
354:         CSR_SIP            = 12'h144,
355:         CSR_SATP           = 12'h180,
356:         // Machine Mode CSRs
357:         CSR_MSTATUS        = 12'h300,
358:         CSR_MISA           = 12'h301,
359:         CSR_MEDELEG        = 12'h302,
360:         CSR_MIDELEG        = 12'h303,
361:         CSR_MIE            = 12'h304,
362:         CSR_MTVEC          = 12'h305,
363:         CSR_MCOUNTEREN     = 12'h306,
364:         CSR_MSCRATCH       = 12'h340,
365:         CSR_MEPC           = 12'h341,
366:         CSR_MCAUSE         = 12'h342,
367:         CSR_MTVAL          = 12'h343,
368:         CSR_MIP            = 12'h344,
369:         CSR_PMPCFG0        = 12'h3A0,
370:         CSR_PMPADDR0       = 12'h3B0,
371:         CSR_MVENDORID      = 12'hF11,
372:         CSR_MARCHID        = 12'hF12,
373:         CSR_MIMPID         = 12'hF13,
374:         CSR_MHARTID        = 12'hF14,
375:         CSR_MCYCLE         = 12'hB00,
376:         CSR_MINSTRET       = 12'hB02,
377:         CSR_DCACHE         = 12'h701,
378:         CSR_ICACHE         = 12'h700,
379: 
380:         CSR_TSELECT        = 12'h7A0,
381:         CSR_TDATA1         = 12'h7A1,
382:         CSR_TDATA2         = 12'h7A2,
383:         CSR_TDATA3         = 12'h7A3,
384:         CSR_TINFO          = 12'h7A4,
385: 
386:         // Debug CSR
387:         CSR_DCSR           = 12'h7b0,
388:         CSR_DPC            = 12'h7b1,
389:         CSR_DSCRATCH0      = 12'h7b2, // optional
390:         CSR_DSCRATCH1      = 12'h7b3, // optional
391:         // Counters and Timers
392:         CSR_CYCLE          = 12'hC00,
393:         CSR_TIME           = 12'hC01,
394:         CSR_INSTRET        = 12'hC02,
395:         // Performance counters
396:         CSR_L1_ICACHE_MISS = PERF_L1_ICACHE_MISS + 12'hC03,
397:         CSR_L1_DCACHE_MISS = PERF_L1_DCACHE_MISS + 12'hC03,
398:         CSR_ITLB_MISS      = PERF_ITLB_MISS      + 12'hC03,
399:         CSR_DTLB_MISS      = PERF_DTLB_MISS      + 12'hC03,
400:         CSR_LOAD           = PERF_LOAD           + 12'hC03,
401:         CSR_STORE          = PERF_STORE          + 12'hC03,
402:         CSR_EXCEPTION      = PERF_EXCEPTION      + 12'hC03,
403:         CSR_EXCEPTION_RET  = PERF_EXCEPTION_RET  + 12'hC03,
404:         CSR_BRANCH_JUMP    = PERF_BRANCH_JUMP    + 12'hC03,
405:         CSR_CALL           = PERF_CALL           + 12'hC03,
406:         CSR_RET            = PERF_RET   
[TRUNCATED]

{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "220:   logic                     flush_commit;
221: 
222:   icache_areq_i_t           icache_areq_ex_cache;
223:   icache_areq_o_t           icache_areq_cache_ex;
224:   icache_dreq_i_t           icache_dreq_if_cache;
225:   icache_dreq_o_t           icache_dreq_cache_if;
226: 
227:   amo_req_t                 amo_req;
228:   amo_resp_t                amo_resp;
229:   logic                     sb_full;
230: 
231:   logic debug_req;
232:   // Disable debug during AMO commit
233:   assign debug_req = debug_req_i & ~amo_valid_commit;
234: 
235:   assign priv_lvl_o = priv_lvl ;
236: 
237:   // ----------------
238:   // DCache <-> *
239:   // ----------------
240:   dcache_req_i_t [2:0]      dcache_req_ports_ex_cache;
241:   dcache_req_o_t [2:0]      dcache_req_ports_cache_ex;
242:   logic                     dcache_commit_wbuffer_empty;
243: 
244:   // --------------
245:   // Frontend
246:   // --------------
247:   frontend i_frontend (
248:     .flush_i             ( flush_ctrl_if                 ), // not entirely correct
249:     .flush_bp_i          ( 1'b0                          ),
250:     .debug_mode_i        ( debug_mode                    ),
251:     .boot_addr_i         ( boot_addr_i                   ),
252:     .icache_dreq_i       ( icache_dreq_cache_if          ),
253:     .icache_dreq_o       ( icache_dreq_if_cache          ),
254:     .resolved_branch_i   ( resolved_branch               ),
255:     .pc_commit_i         ( pc_commit                     ),
256:     .set_pc_commit_i     ( set_pc_ctrl_pcgen             ),
257:     .set_debug_pc_i      ( set_debug_pc                  ),
258:     .epc_i               ( epc_commit_pcgen              ),
259:     .eret_i              ( eret                          ),
260:     .trap_vector_base_i  ( trap_vector_base_commit_pcgen ),
261:     .ex_valid_i          ( ex_commit.valid               ),
262:     .fetch_entry_o       ( fetch_entry_if_id             ),
263:     .fetch_entry_valid_o ( fetch_valid_if_id             ),
264:     .fetch_ack_i         ( decode_ack_id_if              ),
265:     .*
266:   );
267: 
268:   // ---------
269:   // ID
270:   // ---------
271:   id_stage id_stage_i (
272:     .flush_i                    ( flush_ctrl_if              ),
273: 
274:     .fetch_entry_i              ( fetch_entry_if_id          ),
275:     .fetch_entry_valid_i        ( fetch_valid_if_id          ),
276:     .decoded_instr_ack_o        ( decode_ack_id_if           ),
277: 
278:     .issue_entry_o              ( issue_entry_id_issue       ),
279:     .issue_entry_valid_o        ( issue_entry_valid_id_issue ),
280:     .is_ctrl_flow_o             ( is_ctrl_fow_id_issue       ),
281:     .issue_instr_ack_i          ( issue_instr_issue_id       ),
282: 
283:     .priv_lvl_i                 ( priv_lvl                   ),
284:     .fs_i                       ( fs                         ),
285:     .frm_i                      ( frm_csr_id_issue_ex        ),
286:     .debug_mode_i               ( debug_mode                 ),
287:     .tvm_i                      ( tvm_csr_id                 ),
288:     .tw_i                       ( tw_csr_id                  ),
289:     .tsr_i                      ( tsr_csr_id                 ),
290:     .*
291:   );
292: 
293:   // ---------
294:   // Issue
295:   // ---------
296:   issue_stage #(
297:     .NR_ENTRIES                 ( NR_SB_ENTRIES                ),
298:     .NR_WB_PORTS                ( NR_WB_PORTS                  )
299:   ) issue_stage_i (
300:     .clk_i,
301:     .rst_ni,
302:     .sb_full_o                  ( sb_full                      ),
303:     .flush_unissued_instr_i     ( flush_unissued_instr_ctrl_id ),
304:     .flush_i                    ( flush_ctrl_id                ),
305:     // ID Stage
306:     .decoded_instr_i            ( issue_entry_id_issue         ),
307:     
[TRUNCATED]

{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "373:     // CSR
374:     .csr_valid_i            ( csr_valid_id_ex             ),
375:     .csr_addr_o             ( csr_addr_ex_csr             ),
376:     .csr_commit_i           ( csr_commit_commit_ex        ), // from commit
377:     // MULT
378:     .mult_valid_i           ( mult_valid_id_ex            ),
379:     // LSU
380:     .lsu_ready_o            ( lsu_ready_ex_id             ),
381:     .lsu_valid_i            ( lsu_valid_id_ex             ),
382: 
383:     .load_result_o          ( load_result_ex_id           ),
384:     .load_trans_id_o        ( load_trans_id_ex_id         ),
385:     .load_valid_o           ( load_valid_ex_id            ),
386:     .load_exception_o       ( load_exception_ex_id        ),
387: 
388:     .store_result_o         ( store_result_ex_id          ),
389:     .store_trans_id_o       ( store_trans_id_ex_id        ),
390:     .store_valid_o          ( store_valid_ex_id           ),
391:     .store_exception_o      ( store_exception_ex_id       ),
392: 
393:     .lsu_commit_i           ( lsu_commit_commit_ex        ), // from commit
394:     .lsu_commit_ready_o     ( lsu_commit_ready_ex_commit  ), // to commit
395:     .no_st_pending_o        ( no_st_pending_ex            ),
396:     // FPU
397:     .fpu_ready_o            ( fpu_ready_ex_id             ),
398:     .fpu_valid_i            ( fpu_valid_id_ex             ),
399:     .fpu_fmt_i              ( fpu_fmt_id_ex               ),
400:     .fpu_rm_i               ( fpu_rm_id_ex                ),
401:     .fpu_frm_i              ( frm_csr_id_issue_ex         ),
402:     .fpu_prec_i             ( fprec_csr_ex                ),
403:     .fpu_trans_id_o         ( fpu_trans_id_ex_id          ),
404:     .fpu_result_o           ( fpu_result_ex_id            ),
405:     .fpu_valid_o            ( fpu_valid_ex_id             ),
406:     .fpu_exception_o        ( fpu_exception_ex_id         ),
407:     .amo_valid_commit_i     ( amo_valid_commit            ),
408:     .amo_req_o              ( amo_req                     ),
409:     .amo_resp_i             ( amo_resp                    ),
410:     // Performance counters
411:     .itlb_miss_o            ( itlb_miss_ex_perf           ),
412:     .dtlb_miss_o            ( dtlb_miss_ex_perf           ),
413:     // Memory Management
414:     .enable_translation_i   ( enable_translation_csr_ex   ), // from CSR
415:     .en_ld_st_translation_i ( en_ld_st_translation_csr_ex ),
416:     .flush_tlb_i            ( flush_tlb_ctrl_ex           ),
417:     .priv_lvl_i             ( priv_lvl                    ), // from CSR
418:     .ld_st_priv_lvl_i       ( ld_st_priv_lvl_csr_ex       ), // from CSR
419:     .sum_i                  ( sum_csr_ex                  ), // from CSR
420:     .mxr_i                  ( mxr_csr_ex                  ), // from CSR
421:     .satp_ppn_i             ( satp_ppn_csr_ex             ), // from CSR
422:     .asid_i                 ( asid_csr_ex                 ), // from CSR
423:     .icache_areq_i          ( icache_areq_cache_ex        ),
424:     .icache_areq_o          ( icache_areq_ex_cache        ),
425:     // DCACHE interfaces
426:     .dcache_req_ports_i     ( dcache_req_ports_cache_ex   ),
427:     .dcache_req_ports_o     ( dcache_req_ports_ex_cache   )
428:   );
429: 
430:   // ---------
431:   // Commit
432:   // ---------
433: 
434:   // we have to make sure that the whole write buffer path is empty before
435:   // used e.g. for fence instructions.
436:   assign no_st_pending_commit = no_st_pending_ex & dcache_commit_wbuffer_empty;
437: 
438:   commit_stage commit_stage_i (
439:     .clk_i,
440:     .rst_ni,
441:     .halt_i                 ( halt_ctrl                     ),
442:     .flush_dcache_i         ( dcache_flush_ctrl_cache       ),
443:     .exception_o            ( ex_commit                     ),
444:     .dirty_fp_state_o       ( di
[TRUNCATED]

520:     .umode_i                ( umode_i                       ),
521:     .ipi_i,
522:     .irq_i,
523:     .time_irq_i,
524:     .*
525:   );
526: 
527:   // ------------------------
528:   // Performance Counters
529:   // ------------------------
530:   perf_counters i_perf_counters (
531:     .clk_i             ( clk_i                  ),
532:     .rst_ni            ( rst_ni                 ),
533:     .debug_mode_i      ( debug_mode             ),
534:     .addr_i            ( addr_csr_perf          ),
535:     .we_i              ( we_csr_perf            ),
536:     .data_i            ( data_csr_perf          ),
537:     .data_o            ( data_perf_csr          ),
538:     .commit_instr_i    ( commit_instr_id_commit ),
539:     .commit_ack_i      ( commit_ack             ),
540: 
541:     .l1_icache_miss_i  ( icache_miss_cache_perf ),
542:     .l1_dcache_miss_i  ( dcache_miss_cache_perf ),
543:     .itlb_miss_i       ( itlb_miss_ex_perf      ),
544:     .dtlb_miss_i       ( dtlb_miss_ex_perf      ),
545:     .sb_full_i         ( sb_full                ),
546:     .if_empty_i        ( ~fetch_valid_if_id     ),
547:     .ex_i              ( ex_commit              ),
548:     .eret_i            ( eret                   ),
549:     .resolved_branch_i ( resolved_branch        )
550:   );
551: 
552:   // ------------
553:   // Controller
554:   // ------------
555:   controller controller_i (
556:     // flush ports
557:     .set_pc_commit_o        ( set_pc_ctrl_pcgen             ),
558:     .flush_unissued_instr_o ( flush_unissued_instr_ctrl_id  ),
559:     .flush_if_o             ( flush_ctrl_if                 ),
560:     .flush_id_o             ( flush_ctrl_id                 ),
561:     .flush_ex_o             ( flush_ctrl_ex                 ),
562:     .flush_tlb_o            ( flush_tlb_ctrl_ex             ),
563:     .flush_dcache_o         ( dcache_flush_ctrl_cache       ),
564:     .flush_dcache_ack_i     ( dcache_flush_ack_cache_ctrl   ),
565: 
566:     .halt_csr_i             ( halt_csr_ctrl                 ),
567:     .halt_o                 ( halt_ctrl                     ),
568:     // control ports
569:     .eret_i                 ( eret                          ),
570:     .ex_valid_i             ( ex_commit.valid               ),
571:     .set_debug_pc_i         ( set_debug_pc                  ),
572:     .flush_csr_i            ( flush_csr_ctrl                ),
573:     .resolved_branch_i      ( resolved_branch               ),
574:     .fence_i_i              ( fence_i_commit_controller     ),
575:     .fence_i                ( fence_commit_controller       ),
576:     .sfence_vma_i           ( sfence_vma_commit_controller  ),
577:     .flush_commit_i         ( flush_commit                  ),
578: 
579:     .flush_icache_o         ( icache_flush_ctrl_cache       ),
580:     .*
581:   );
582: 
583:   // -------------------
584:   // Cache Subsystem
585:   // -------------------
586: 
587: `ifdef PITON_ARIANE
588:   // this is a cache subsystem that is compatible with OpenPiton
589:   serpent_cache_subsystem #(
590:     .CachedAddrBeg        ( CachedAddrBeg ),
591:     .CachedAddrEnd        ( CachedAddrEnd ),
592:     .SwapEndianess        ( SwapEndianess )
593:   ) i_cache_subsystem (
594:     // to D$
595:     .clk_i                 ( clk_i                       ),
596:     .rst_ni                ( rst_ni                      ),
597:     // I$
598:     .icache_en_i           ( icache_en_csr               ),
599:     .icache_flush_i        ( icache_flush_ctrl_cache     ),

506:                 riscv::CSR_RET,
507:                 riscv::CSR_MIS_PREDICT: begin
508:                                         perf_data_o = csr_wdata;
509:                                         perf_we_o   = 1'b1;
510:                 end
511:                 default: update_access_exception = 1'b1;
512:             endcase
513:         end
514: 
515:         mstatus_d.sxl  = riscv::XLEN_64;
516:         mstatus_d.uxl  = riscv::XLEN_64;
517: 
518:         // mark the floating point extension register as dirty
519:         if (FP_PRESENT && (dirty_fp_state_csr || dirty_fp_state_i)) begin
520:             mstatus_d.fs = riscv::Dirty;
521:         end
522: 
523:         // write the floating point status register
524:         if (csr_write_fflags_i) begin
525:             fcsr_d.fflags = csr_wdata_i[4:0] | fcsr_q.fflags;
526:         end
527:         // ---------------------
528:         // External Interrupts
529:         // ---------------------
530:         // Machine Mode External Interrupt Pending
531:         mip_d[riscv::IRQ_M_EXT] = irq_i[0];
532:         // Machine software interrupt
533:         mip_d[riscv::IRQ_M_SOFT] = ipi_i;
534:         // Timer interrupt pending, coming from platform timer
535:         mip_d[riscv::IRQ_M_TIMER] = time_irq_i;
536: 
537:         // -----------------------
538:         // Manage Exception Stack
539:         // -----------------------
540:         // update exception CSRs
541:         // we got an exception update cause, pc and stval register
542:         trap_to_priv_lvl = riscv::PRIV_LVL_M;
543:         // Exception is taken and we are not in debug mode
544:         // exceptions in debug mode don't update any fields
545:         if (!debug_mode_q && ex_i.valid) begin
546:             // do not flush, flush is reserved for CSR writes with side effects
547:             flush_o   = 1'b0;
548:             // figure out where to trap to
549:             // a m-mode trap might be delegated if we are taking it in S mode
550:             // first figure out if this was an exception or an interrupt e.g.: look at bit 63
551:             // the cause register can only be 6 bits long (as we only support 64 exceptions)
552:             if ((ex_i.cause[63] && mideleg_q[ex_i.cause[5:0]]) ||
553:                 (~ex_i.cause[63] && medeleg_q[ex_i.cause[5:0]])) begin
554:                 // traps never transition from a more-privileged mode to a less privileged mode
555:                 // so if we are already in M mode, stay there
556:                 trap_to_priv_lvl = (priv_lvl_o == riscv::PRIV_LVL_M) ? riscv::PRIV_LVL_M : riscv::PRIV_LVL_S;
557:             end
558: 
559:             // trap to supervisor mode
560:             if (trap_to_priv_lvl == riscv::PRIV_LVL_S) begin
561:                 // update sstatus
562:                 mstatus_d.sie  = 1'b0;
563:                 mstatus_d.spie = mstatus_q.sie;
564:                 // this can either be user or supervisor mode
565:                 mstatus_d.spp  = priv_lvl_q[0];

848:         // Privilege Check
849:         // -----------------
850:         // if we are reading or writing, check for the correct privilege level this has
851:         // precedence over interrupts
852:         if (csr_we || csr_read) begin
853:             //if ((riscv::priv_lvl_t'(priv_lvl_o & csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl) != csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl)) begin
854:             if ((riscv::priv_lvl_t'(priv_lvl_o & csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl) != csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl) && !(csr_addr.address==riscv::CSR_MEPC)) begin   
855:                 csr_exception_o.cause = riscv::ILLEGAL_INSTR;
856:                 csr_exception_o.valid = 1'b1;
857:             end
858:             // check access to debug mode only CSRs
859:             if (csr_addr_i[11:4] == 8'h7b && !debug_mode_q) begin
860:                 csr_exception_o.cause = riscv::ILLEGAL_INSTR;
861:                 csr_exception_o.valid = 1'b1;
862:             end
863:         end
864:         // we got an exception in one of the processes above
865:         // throw an illegal instruction exception
866:         if (update_access_exception || read_access_exception) begin
867:             csr_exception_o.cause = riscv::ILLEGAL_INSTR;
868:             // we don't set the tval field as this will be set by the commit stage
869:             // this spares the extra wiring from commit to CSR and back to commit
870:             csr_exception_o.valid = 1'b1;
871:         end
